title: symphytum officinale subsp. officinale
intro: Symphytum officinale subsp. officinale, also known as comfrey, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, sprains, and other musculoskeletal issues. Comfrey is believed to promote tissue repair, reduce pain and swelling, and support overall health by providing essential nutrients and minerals. Herbal preparations of comfrey, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, can be used topically to soothe and heal skin conditions, while also being ingested as a tea or added to food as a nutritional supplement.
